mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, which drives corporate earnings expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of months with positive payroll growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An effective federal funds rate above 5.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that raises the cost of capital and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in greater than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in retail sales and decrease in the unemployment rate amplifies household income-consumption feedback, generating stronger corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days than either signal in isolation.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between the interaction term and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1,800 thousand units during a late-cycle expansion signal an overbuilt residential sector; the ensuing contraction in construction activity and housing wealth produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of instances when HOUST exceeds 1,800 during late-cycle expansions over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent increase in the federal funds rate and the consumer price index signals front-loaded monetary tightening that elevates equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between the joint FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL signal and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede slower consumer spending and weaker corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE rises more than 0.2pp over three months during any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP signals expanding aggregate demand that underpins broad-based corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of quarters following positive real GDP growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production reflects rising manufacturing output and capacity utilization; higher utilization boosts operating leverage and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of months with month-over-month industrial production acceleration over any 3-year rolling window.
